makeAheadNotes: Gumbo, beans, and stews improve after resting; flavors deepen overnight.
Store rice separately; reheat stews gently and cook rice fresh when possible.
Dark roux brings depth but thickens less; plan simmer time accordingly.

identity.summary: Louisiana Cajun home cooking built on the Trinity (onion-celery-bell pepper), roux, smoked meats, seafood, and layered spice—often in one-pot dishes.
pillars: Roux for body and deep flavor

bases.label: The Trinity
bases.notes: Sweat until soft; add garlic near the end to avoid burning.

bases.label: Roux
bases.notes: Blonde → peanut butter → dark chocolate. Darker roux = more flavor, less thickening.

heat.notes: Increase heat gradually; taste late in cooking after reduction.

proteinPatterns.label: Smoked backbone protein
proteinPatterns.notes: Provides depth for gumbo, beans, and many one-pot dishes.

proteinPatterns.label: Seafood added late
proteinPatterns.notes: Add near end to prevent overcooking; finish with herbs/hot sauce.

styleNotes.label: Tomatoes in gumbo
styleNotes.notes: Often excluded in Cajun gumbo (more common in Creole-leaning versions).

styleNotes.label: Cream-heavy sauces
styleNotes.notes: Not typical; richness comes from roux/butter and reduction.
